description: Spur gear on a stepped shaft, standing upright along Z axis, centered at origin in XY. Three-section stepped shaft with integrated 20-tooth spur gear at middle section, through bore, and keyway slot.
components: Lower journal cylinder (D=15mm, Z=0 to Z=20)
Middle gear seat cylinder (D=20mm, Z=20 to Z=35)
Upper journal cylinder (D=15mm, Z=35 to Z=55)
Spur gear teeth (20-tooth star-polygon profile, tip R=22mm, root R=18mm, Z=20 to Z=35)
Keyway slot (3mm wide, 3mm deep radially, +X side, Z=20 to Z=35)
notes: 1. The gear tooth profile uses a star-polygon approximation: 40 vertices alternating between tip radius 22mm and root radius 18mm, evenly spaced at 9-degree angular intervals (360/40=9 degrees per vertex), creating 20 tooth tips and 20 root valleys. The polygon is extruded from Z=20 to Z=35. 2. The shaft is built as union of three cylinders; the gear profile is unioned at the middle section. 3. The through bore (cylinder D=8mm, full Z length) is subtracted from the entire assembly. 4. The keyway is a rectangular box slot: width 3mm in Y direction, centered on X axis (Y from -1.5 to +1.5), extending in X from +4mm to +7mm (radial depth 3mm outward from bore wall at R=4), and spanning Z=20 to Z=35. Subtract this box from the assembly. 5. Ensure the star-polygon gear vertices are correctly oriented so tooth tips align symmetrically; first vertex at angle=0 (along +X) at tip radius 22mm recommended. 6. Volume estimate accounts for shaft minus bore minus keyway plus gear tooth material above the gear seat cylinder baseline.
